Notice Of Motion And Motion To Specially Set Hearing And Accept Filing Nunc Pro Tunc For Plaintiffs Motion For Summary Judgment, Or Alternatively, For Summary Adjudication; Or Alternatively, For Trial Continuance
Off Calendar Aug-25-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF PACIFIC STATES BUILDING, LP Notice Of Motion And Motion To Specially Set Hearing And Accept Filing Nunc Pro Tunc For Plaintiffs Motion For Summary Judgment, Or Alternatively, For Summary Adjudication; Or Alternatively, For Trial Continuance

Appearances (by Zoom):
Mark Desantis, Esq. 415-567-5212 Gianunzio & Desantis LLP for Plaintiff.
Enoch Wong, Esq. 415-837-3101 Fife Law LLP for Defendant. (in-person)

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF PACIFIC STATES BUILDING, LP'S Motion To Specially Set Hearing And Accept Filing Nunc Pro Tunc For Plaintiffs Motion For Summary Judgment, Or Alternatively, For Summary Adjudication; Or Alternatively, For Trial Continuance

Plaintiff's motion to specially set summary judgment hearing is denied and its request for a trial continuance is denied without prejudice. First, this a real property/landlord-tenant matter and such law and motion filings (save trial continuances) are heard in department 501 per SF Local Rule 8.10A1. Second, at this point, re-setting the summary judgment hearing for September 21, 2003, would effectively be an order shortening time on the summary judgment motion. The court lacks the authority to enter such an order unless the parties stipulate to that relief. (See McMahon v. Superior Court (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 112, 118 ["[W]e hold that, in light of the express statutory language, trial courts do not have authority to shorten the minimum notice period for summary judgment hearings."]; Urshan v. Musicians' Credit Union (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 758, 763 [same].) Third, given the above, plaintiff needs to obtain a trial continuance to have its motion for summary judgment heard. The presiding judge in department 206 hears such motions pursuant to SF Local Rule 6.0B.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Mtn To Compel Production Of Docs And Depo Of Ziegler
Off Calendar Jul-31-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. MICHELLE L. LANDRY, Esq. of Vedder Price (CA), LLP (Tel: 415-749-9500) and M. DAVID DESANTIS, Esq. of Gianunzio & DeSantis LLP (Tel: 415-567-5212) for plaintiff/cross defendant Pacific States Building, LP;
Atty. ENOCH WANG, Esq. of Fife Law, LLP (Tel: 415-837-3101) for defendant/cross-complainant iBelieveInSwordfish Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT IBELIEVEINSWORDFISH INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF
DOCUMENTS AND DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF PARTY AFFILIATED WITNESS JANICE ZIEGLER.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with a modification, as follows:

Motion is granted, in part. Ms. Ziegler shall submit to oral deposition on or before August 31, 2023. The deposition may be conducted remotely due to Ms. Zeigler's medical condition and travel schedule. Counsel for the parties shall meet and confer, in good faith, to select a mutually convenient date for the deposition. PSB shall produce responsive documents to defense counsel at least 5 days before the deposition, except as to requests relating to PSB's financial condition, tax returns, other properties, and leases with other tenants, as well as with respect to information beyond the last five years. As to these requests, the motion is denied, without prejudice, because defendant has not set forth specific facts showing good cause justifying the discovery of this information in the context of the parties' lease dispute. Counsel are required to meet and confer in good faith to narrow scope of any requests in dispute prior to filing any further motions.

PSB shall also produce the rent roll/tenant roster of other units at 445 Bush Street, San Francisco from September 2020 through May 2023.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order to the opposing side for approval as to form, and then submit the proposed order by email to the pro tem judge at aaron@minnisandsmallets.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Aaron Minnis; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
